seoTitle: Clemton Park vs Clergate — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Clemton Park vs Clergate: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Clemton Park and Clergate.
heading: Clemton Park vs Clergate — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Clemton Park, New South Wales and Clergate, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clemton Park has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Clergate (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Clemton Park or Clergate safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Clemton Park has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Clergate scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Clemton Park scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Clergate scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Clemton Park or Clergate?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clemton Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
